derived_from: Generalized patterns extracted from prior water-infrastructure PBQ examples. Project names, client names, locations, document filenames, and site-specific facts have been removed.
purpose: Reusable bidder-intelligence playbook for DBO/EPC/O&M water infrastructure tenders. Use it to generate practical human-style PBQs in addition to deep commercial, legal, and cross-document risk queries.

generation_modes.instruction: Find hidden commercial, legal, technical, payment, escalation, O&M, and cross-document risks.
Also generate obvious bidder clarification rows that a tender engineer would raise even if they are not the highest-risk item.

preferred_phrases: Please include the same in BOQ.
structure: Briefly quote or paraphrase the tender requirement.
State the bidder's understanding or the gap.
Ask for one concrete employer action: provide, confirm, clarify, revise, include in BOQ, define responsibility, or amend clause.

industry_norms.norm: For similar municipal water tenders, bidders commonly request DLP reduction from 24 months to 12 months after completion certificate.
High running-bill security deposit deductions materially hurt contractor cash flow; bidders commonly request about 3% cap or release against bank guarantee.
O&M security for a full 10-year amount can be excessive; bidders commonly request annualized, reducing, or lower percentage security.
Bidders commonly request mobilization advance to be interest-free for project cash-flow efficiency.
Monthly O&M payment is often preferred over quarterly or annual payment for operating cash flow.
Fixed annual escalation may not reflect real labor, chemical, power, fuel, consumables, or statutory wage movement; request a standard escalation formula.
Changes in tax, law, statutory interpretation, or technical standards after bid base date should generally be compensable or treated as variation.
Bidders should not carry unbounded subsurface, hydrological, obstruction, pollutant, or manmade physical condition risk without relief/variation mechanism.

standard_missing_data_checklist: AutoCAD/DWG files for all tender drawings and DPR drawings
Complete DPR and annexures referenced by drawings/specifications
Overall layout/key plan marking all existing and proposed units
Route alignment plans and L-sections for all pipelines
Geotechnical/soil investigation reports with bore logs and lab data
Topographic survey drawings, raw survey data, contour drawings, and control points
Hydrological study, HFL/LWL, scour, river velocity, and flood data
Hydraulic design basis, system head curves, surge analysis, pump curves, and design calculations
Utility mapping/as-built drawings for underground and overhead utilities
Land/ROW/forest/rail/road/canal crossing permissions and responsibility status
Site access, approach road, working space, barricading, and sheet-piling constraints
Existing asset condition assessment, asset registers, rehabilitation baseline, and handover protocol
commercial_risk_checklist: Whether BOQ items are all-inclusive or payable as variation
Whether new works are lump sum while rehabilitation/pipe works are item-rate
Payment milestone percentages that do not total 100%
Trial-run/commissioning payment linkage and who bears repeated trial costs
RA bill frequency and billing break-up mechanism
Secured advance availability, recovery, and treatment in price adjustment
Mobilization advance amount, interest, BG format, and recovery
Security deposit percentage, release timing, and replacement by BG
Performance security amount/validity for capex and O&M
Buy-back/scrap value reasonableness, inventory, condition, and valuation basis
Base indices, base date, source indices, and component weights for price adjustment
Tax, GST, TDS, duties, statutory levy, and change-in-law treatment
legal_risk_checklist: Liability cap and exclusion of indirect/consequential losses
Liquidated damages cap and sole-remedy wording
Employer approval delay and deemed approval
Employer default and contractor right to terminate
Payment upon termination, including demobilization, profit/loss, securities return, and work value
Termination for convenience should be mutual/fair or compensated
Change in law / change in technical standards adjustment
Insurance coverage details, waiver of subrogation, uninsurable risks, and deductibles
technical_execution_checklist: Approach bridge dimensions, GAD, design criteria, and geotech/topography
Intake screens: number, standby, opening, bar thickness, MOC, manual/mechanical operation, conveyor, bins
Raw-water/clear-water pump head, discharge, motor rating, efficiency, and duty/standby conflicts
Surge vessel type, sizing, analysis, and conventional vs diaphragm conflicts
Pipeline route lengths, interconnection points, crossings, thrust/anchor blocks, bends, supports, and anti-buoyancy
Nallah/stream/road/rail/canal crossings and required construction methodology
SCADA/PLC architecture, redundancy, protocols, server integration, API integration, instrumentation quantity, and external authority/platform interface
HT line/substation route, land, metering, feeder bay, O&M responsibility, and payment schedule
WTP process design criteria, raw water quality data, chemical/fuel/power assumptions, and sludge/backwash scope
o_and_m_checklist: O&M start date for new and existing/rehabilitated assets
PG test, commissioning certificate, completion certificate, and section-wise handover conditions
Whether O&M of existing facilities is required during construction
Major maintenance threshold and routine-vs-major repair split
Existing asset repair/replacement responsibility and payment mechanism
Manpower requirement reasonableness and bidder flexibility
Power, fuel, chemicals, consumables, sludge, waste disposal, and statutory wage escalation
O&M performance security and release/reduction basis
Condition assessment and baseline for handover/takeover
coverage_rules: Run both deep_risk_discovery and manual_pbq_coverage modes before finalizing.
For each tender section/category, ask what a practical bidder needs to price, design, construct, operate, insure, schedule, or legally accept the work.
Do not reject a useful practical clarification merely because it is not Critical; keep Medium rows when they protect price, scope, design, schedule, or responsibility.
Prefer exact page/clause references from evidence. If not available, use '-' rather than inventing.
Deduplicate only if the employer action is the same. Same broad topic with different required action should remain separate.
Use the playbook as reusable tender intelligence, not as project fact. A query still needs project evidence or a material absence after search.
